source: Label 309 structural-validator rejection corpus — one pinned code set per Part A failure mode
description: Each case pins a reassembled record body (cbor_hex) and the exact expected_error_codes the structural validator MUST emit; expected_info_codes pins info-severity tags where applicable; an empty expected_error_codes marks a contrast case that MUST validate. validator_options, where present, names the deployment configuration the expectation assumes.

vectors.name: sig-entry-extra-field
vectors.note: A sigs[i] map carrying any key beyond the closed {cose_sign1, ?cose_key} set is rejected.

vectors.name: sig-entry-missing-cose-sign1
vectors.note: A sigs[i] map without the REQUIRED cose_sign1 key is rejected.

vectors.name: sig-cose-attached-payload
vectors.note: COSE_Sign1[2] must be CBOR null (detached). An attached payload — even zero-length — is rejected; a producer chaining a CIP-30 signData result must replace the payload with null before embedding.

vectors.name: sig-kid-cose-key-conflict
vectors.note: A 32-byte protected-header kid (path 1) and an inline cose_key (path 2) are mutually exclusive in one sigs[i] entry.

vectors.name: sig-private-key-leaked
vectors.note: An inline cose_key carrying the private scalar d (COSE_Key label -4) is rejected; private-key material must never reach the permanent ledger.

vectors.name: enc-empty-hashes
vectors.note: An empty hashes map violates the non-empty map rule and leaves the ciphertext bound to no plaintext digest.

vectors.name: enc-noncontent-hashes
vectors.note: An enc-bearing item whose only hashes key is unregistered carries no content-hash commitment; the two codes co-fire on the same item.

vectors.name: crit-base-key
vectors.note: A crit[] entry naming a base key is rejected; only extension keys may be critical.

vectors.name: crit-absent-field
vectors.note: A crit[] entry must name a key present in the record map.

vectors.name: crit-duplicate-entries
vectors.note: Duplicate crit[] entries are rejected. supportedCriticalExtensions includes x-note so the duplication rule is isolated from the unsupported-extension rule.

vectors.name: crit-unsupported-extension
vectors.note: A well-formed crit[] entry naming an extension outside the validator's supportedCriticalExtensions set (default: empty) fails the record by design.

vectors.name: ext-key-single-trailing-newline
vectors.note: An extension-style key containing any control character (here a trailing U+000A) is inadmissible regardless of regex match.

vectors.name: schema-unknown-field-typo
vectors.note: A top-level key outside the base set that matches neither extension-key namespace (a typo of a base name) is rejected.

vectors.name: merkle-leaf-count-2pow32
vectors.note: 4294967296 = 2^32 exceeds the pinned 1 .. 2^32-1 range.

vectors.name: merkle-leaf-count-2pow53-plus-1
vectors.note: 2^53 + 1 pins exact-integer handling: an implementation decoding through a lossy float would round this to 2^53 and could disagree about the boundary; the value must be handled exactly and rejected as out of range.

vectors.name: uri-scheme-upper-ar-valid
vectors.note: The scheme is case-folded before matching; the txid body is matched verbatim.

vectors.name: uri-https-forbidden
vectors.note: The fetch set is closed to the content-addressed schemes {ar://, ipfs://}.

vectors.name: enc-aead-aes-256-cbc
vectors.note: An aead naming an unauthenticated cipher mode is rejected with the hazard-specific code in every role, never degraded to an opaque envelope.

vectors.name: enc-nonce-12-bytes
vectors.note: chacha20-poly1305-stream64k registers a 24-byte enc.nonce.

vectors.name: enc-slots-mac-without-slots
vectors.note: slots_mac with no slots violates two independent cross-field rules: the MAC binds nothing (ENC_SLOTS_REQUIRED) and the envelope carries no key path (ENC_NO_KEY_PATH). Both are collected.

vectors.name: enc-exclusivity-both-paths
vectors.note: The slots path and the passphrase path are mutually exclusive in one envelope.

vectors.name: enc-slot-shape-hybrid-under-x25519
vectors.note: A kem_ct-shaped slot under enc.kem = x25519 violates the KEM-selected closed 2-key slot map.

vectors.name: enc-slot-shape-classical-under-hybrid
vectors.note: An epk-shaped slot under enc.kem = mlkem768x25519 violates the KEM-selected closed 2-key slot map.

vectors.name: enc-slot-stray-key
vectors.note: A slot carrying any key beyond the closed 2-key map for its KEM is rejected.

vectors.name: kem-ct-length-1119
vectors.note: slot.kem_ct is a single 1120-byte byte string (ML-KEM-768 ct 1088 || X25519 ephemeral 32).

vectors.name: enc-slots-duplicate-epk-x25519
vectors.note: Two slots sharing one epk would derive one KEK for two wraps; within-record KEM-material duplication is rejected before any cryptographic step.

vectors.name: passphrase-valid-floor
vectors.note: A passphrase envelope at exactly the Argon2id floor (m 65536, t 3, p 1) with a 16-byte salt is structurally valid.

vectors.name: passphrase-params-unknown-subfield
vectors.note: params is a closed map of exactly {m, t, p}.

vectors.name: passphrase-param-above-uint32
vectors.note: An Argon2id parameter above the pinned 0 .. 2^32-1 wire range is a range violation, distinct from the parameter floor code.

vectors.name: passphrase-params-exceed-policy
vectors.note: Policy-dependent: the upper parameter ceiling is a deployment-pinned denial-of-service backstop, not a wire rule. This vector pins the code only under the stated validator_options ceiling (m 8388608 KiB = 8 GiB exceeds the 2 GiB ceiling); a validator configured with no ceiling accepts the record.

vectors.name: passphrase-salt-missing
vectors.note: An absent passphrase.salt carries the same code as a wrong-typed one: the salt's shape is a byte string with its own length refinements, so its absence is a type violation of that shape, not a generic missing-field condition.

vectors.name: record-root-bstr
vectors.note: A record body that decodes to a CBOR byte string where the grammar requires the top-level text-keyed map is rejected with a single type mismatch at the root; a validator must never read the byte string's indices as map keys.

vectors.name: items-entry-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where an items[] entry map is required yields exactly one type mismatch at the entry, never a per-byte-index unknown-field spray plus missing-required fields.

vectors.name: items-entry-bstr-beside-wrong-v
vectors.note: The byte-string-at-map-position violation is a shape-gate issue and co-fires with sibling shape-gate issues elsewhere in the record (here the v literal); only the domain pass is foreclosed.

vectors.name: merkle-entry-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where a merkle[] entry map is required yields exactly one type mismatch at the entry.

vectors.name: sigs-entry-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where a sigs[] entry map is required violates the sig-entry closed-map rule, which owns every shape violation inside a sigs[] entry.

vectors.name: item-hashes-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where the hashes map is required yields exactly one type mismatch at items[i].hashes.

vectors.name: item-enc-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where the enc envelope map is required yields exactly one type mismatch at items[i].enc; the rejection precedes the typed-vs-opaque dispatch, so no envelope reading applies.

vectors.name: enc-slot-bstr-forecloses-nonce-rule
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where a recipient-slot map is required violates the slot shape, and a failed envelope shape gate forecloses the envelope's domain rules: the 12-byte nonce in this body must NOT co-fire NONCE_LENGTH_MISMATCH.

vectors.name: enc-passphrase-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where the passphrase block map is required yields exactly one type mismatch at enc.passphrase.

vectors.name: enc-passphrase-params-bstr
vectors.note: A CBOR byte string where the argon2id params map is required yields exactly one type mismatch at enc.passphrase.params.
